zotero.abstractNote: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning (HRL) aims to solve long-horizon tasks by decomposing them into subtasks. A common challenge is the definition of subgoals and the coordination between the high-level policy, which selects subgoals, and the low-level policy, which executes them. We propose Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning with Timed Subgoals (HiTS), a novel HRL framework that introduces a temporal constraint on subgoals. By predicting not only the desired state but also the time horizon for reaching it, the high-level policy gains explicit control over the temporal abstraction. We show that this simple extension significantly improves the stability and performance of HRL. HiTS achieves state-of-the-art results on a variety of challenging continuous control tasks, including sparse-reward environments and long-horizon navigation tasks.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 34
